macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). US stocks closed modestly higher Friday, with the S&P 500 adding 0.47% to 754.95, as a pullback in oil prices and a strong SK Hynix Nasdaq debut buoyed sentiment. Overnight, U.S.-Iran tensions escalated again with fresh military exchanges and President Trump warning it 'will get much worse,' while Fed minutes revealed officials openly weighing rate hikes ahead of next week's critical CPI report. The June consumer inflation print on Tuesday is now the primary macro catalyst — a hot reading would cement a rate-hike bias and further squeeze tech multiples, while a cooler number could revive the soft-landing narrative and unlock the pile of cash on the sidelines.
commodities_note: WTI slipped 1.35% to $71.41 on Friday, reversing some of the week's Hormuz-driven spike, but the Strait remains a hair-trigger. Gold eased 0.62% to $4,104 as real yields held firm; the metal is stuck between safe-haven bids and the gravitational pull of a 4.57% US10Y. Watch the CPI print — a dovish surprise could push gold back toward $4,200, while a hot number would likely test the $4,000 support.

writeup.headline: Promoted to AVOID — Meta's in-house chip and Compute rent-out plan breaks the neocloud thesis.
writeup.what_to_do: Meta, CoreWeave's largest customer ($21B commitment), is putting its own AI chip into production in September (per Reuters, Jul 9) and reportedly planning a competing 'Meta Compute' GPU rental service. This directly attacks the utilization-pricing moat of the neocloud model. CEO selling (~$31M under a 10b5-1 plan) adds noise, but the structural break is real. Avoid until CoreWeave demonstrates that its non-Meta backlog ($99B+) can absorb the in-housing shift — the Q2 print (expected Aug 18) is the first chance to clear the bar.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if Meta in-housing threat vs backlog growth — the falsifiable test (H2 2026 (Q2 print expected Aug 18)) is delayed, cancelled, or underwhelms market expectations.

The 6.6:1 headline R:R is distorted by a 3.1% stop; the wide-stop R:R to $502.78 structural support is 1.02:1 — no edge. Wait for earnings (Aug 5) and a confirmed design-win disclosure before allocating capital.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 51x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are deep into CAUTION hard-block territory with RSI readings above 80 and extensions >+11% from 50-day averages. The USD funding and S$NEER appreciation headwinds keep NIM relief firmly out of reach — this is a glidepath higher, not an entry point. Watch for a pullback to the mid-60s SGD levels as the first actionable reset.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally neutral — extended, awaiting NIM relief.
singapore.key_development: Cluster-wide extension with no new rate or MAS catalyst; US10Y stuck above 4.20% keeps the dual NIM headwind intact.

semiconductors.summary: Semis are a mixed bag heading into earnings: NVDA reclaims the 50-day but on weak volume, ASML and TSM face near-term earnings binaries, and AVGO/LITE are hard-blocked after steep runs. The Meta in-house chip announcement is a structural headwind for merchant GPU names but a capex tailwind for memory and substrate plays — the cluster is splitting, not rising together. Tread carefully ahead of CPI and the big semi prints.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically cautious — near-term binaries and divergences dominate.
semiconductors.key_development: Meta's Iris AI chip entering production in September (Reuters) drives a wedge between custom-silicon beneficiaries (AVGO, TSM) and merchant GPU exposed names (NVDA, AMD). CRWV thesis broken on Meta Compute.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is rangebound, with all four names on HOLD or CAUTION. The OpenAI-Microsoft dependency deepens with GPT 5.6 designated as the Copilot foundation, keeping MSFT's Azure AI quality in question. Google and Amazon have earnings in 11-19 days — the lack of near-term catalyst keeps this cluster in a wait-and-see mode.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — AI capex confirmed, but MSFT credit-circularity risk remains.
big_tech.key_development: GPT 5.6 becomes 'preferred model' for MSFT Copilot, tightening the OpenAI dependency even as MSFT pivots to own models — a contradictory signal.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: LITE's NVDA-funded fab buildout is the bull case; COHR holds the matching $2B NVIDIA photonics partnership (per NVDA IR, March 2) and is ramping InP capacity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR retests its 50-day on the next photonics sector pullback, particularly after ASML or LITE earnings.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Tower Semi's 5M PIC shipment milestone (per Simply Wall St., Jul 10) confirms MRVL as the key buyer — optical networking demand is real.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor MRVL into its next earnings for optical segment revenue acceleration above $2B quarterly run-rate; then revisit.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: ANET's 1.6Tbps AI switching platform gaining traction with Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle (per Simply Wall St., Jul 11) links directly to the hyperscaler capex cycle and photonics buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Interesting if ANET pulls back 8-10% from current levels and holds the 50-day, especially after MSFT or Meta earnings.

active_risks: US-Iran military conflict: Overnight exchange of fire, Strait of Hormuz traffic near standstill, Trump warns escalation — oil supply risk persists.
China helium export ban: Effective immediately, threatens semiconductor manufacturing gas supply chain (per NY Post, Jul 10), broadening the tech cold war beyond chips.
Fed rate-hike pivot: Minutes reveal 'most' officials see scenarios requiring policy firming; June CPI on Jul 14 is the binary — a hot print would hike odds meaningfully.
US-China trade/tech escalation: Bipartisan push to ban Chinese EVs, critical minerals export controls, and now helium — the trade war is expanding into industrial inputs.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $71.41 and gold at $4,104 reflect a tension between Hormuz supply risk and the rate-hike overhang. Semis remain skittish: AVGO and LITE hard-blocked on momentum, while NVDA's weak-volume rally reflects a market unwilling to commit into CPI. The Singapore banks are extended but benefiting from higher-for-longer rates despite NIM volume pressure — a precarious balance.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US and Iran exchanged fire overnight after Trump declared the ceasefire 'over' (per Haaretz, Jul 9). China announced an immediate helium export ban, citing US-Iran conflict as trigger (per NY Post, Jul 10).

base.description: Stalemate persists: Iran keeps Hormuz tense but avoids full blockade; oil holds $65-75. Fed holds rates with a hiking bias. Semis grind sideways with earnings volatility, Singapore banks stay elevated on rate support. AVGO/LITE cool off.

optimistic.description: CPI comes in cool (<4.0% YoY), US-Iran ceasefire reactivates, WTI falls below $60. Rate-hike bets collapse, tech multiples reflate. NVDA/AMD rally, AVGO/LITE reset block, SG banks pull back to buyable levels.

pessimistic.description: CPI spikes above 4.5%, Fed signals a July hike. Hormuz escalates to blockade, WTI surges above $85. Semis sell off, AVGO/LITE momentum breaks further, BigTech corrects. SG banks give back all recent gains.

wildcard.description: A sudden US-Iran ground conflict or Hormuz mining disrupts 20% of global oil flow; WTI spikes >$100. Safe havens surge; semis and BigTech enter a risk-off cascade. The entire watchlist goes into a drawdown.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until the July CPI report clears the rate-hike fog. Selective entry on a pullback in semis post-earnings only if VIX stays below 18.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 7 of 29 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, and the looming June CPI report and back-to-back ASML/TSM earnings make entry timing precarious. The right posture is patience — let the binaries clear before deploying fresh capital.

contrarian_candidates.watching_for: Hong Kong listing closure or Chinese government shipment permit clarity — then a bounce at $53.36 with volume.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Headline CPI drops to 3.9-4.0% YoY, core eases, rate-hike odds collapse, tech multiples reflate.

AMD → WATCH (extension block could soften if rally broadens)
PLTR → WATCH (PE compression still a concern, but growth narrative strengthened)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: CPI comes in at 4.5%+ YoY, Fed hardens hike language, semis and BigTech sell off.
bearish_impact: AVGO → remains blocked (momentum chase could accelerate downside)
SG banks → remain blocked (higher-for-longer rates sustain extension premium)
